However, there are two concerns: (1) The author list uses '...' to indicate truncation, which is unusual in a formal citation and should be replaced with the complete author list. (2) The remaining bibliographic details (title, journal, volume, issue, pages, DOI, year) all appear correct and match the published paper in Nature.
